data.title: Nasdaq Hits Record, Fueled by Intel Rally — WSJ
data.description:
By Caitlin McCabe

Intel helped brighten a market clouded by war anxiety, pushing Nasdaq to its fifth record close of the year.

Shares of the chip maker surged 24%, hitting a new record high for the first time since 2000 after the company reported higher sales and raised its guidance amid a resurgence in demand for its CPUs. The gain, Intel's biggest in 38 years, added to the U.S. government's windfall on its 2025 investment.

Intel was the best performer in the the S&P 500, which rose 0.8% and also closed at a record. Other tech stocks also showed strength, with Nvidia rising 4.3% to its first record since October Amazon.com moved 3.5% higher.

Other parts of the market struggled. The Dow industrials slipped 0.2%., and six out of the 11 S&P sectors declined including financial and health-care stocks.

The Justice Department meanwhile said it would end its criminal investigation of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, clearing the obstacle that has stalled Kevin Warsh's confirmation as his successor. Treasury yields were slightly lower, with the 2-year note yielding 3.775%.

Concern over Middle East peace talks have weighed on markets. Friday, a White House official said Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will go to Islamabad for talks with Iranian officials. Oil prices ended the day mixed with Brent crude adding 0.2% to $105.33 a barrel and U.S. benchmark crude-oil slipping 1.5% to $94.40.

Anxiety about the war contributed to the grimmest consumer sentiment on record in April, a long-running survey showed.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Lee Enterprises Names Bekke CEO, Rinehults CFO on Permanent Basis
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Lee Enterprises appointed Nathan Bekke as chief executive officer and Josh Rinehults as finance chief on a permanent basis after they served as interim picks in their respective roles.

Bekke became interim CEO at the end of 2025, after private equity billionaire David Hoffmann signed a deal to take a majority stake of up to $50 million in the company, and former CEO Kevin Mowbray announced his retirement.

"Following a comprehensive nationwide search, the board is confident that the right leadership for Lee's future was already in place," Hoffmann, who became chair of the board through his investment, said Friday.

"Nathan and Josh have demonstrated exceptional leadership, deep industry expertise, and a clear vision during their time in their interim roles," he added. "Their ability to execute, inspire our teams, and position Lee for long-term growth made this decision."

Bekke joined Lee in 1988, and had served as chief operating officer beginning in June 2025 prior to his promotion. Rinehults joined the company following its 2020 acquisition of BH Media Group.

Hoffmann's firm, Hoffmann Family of Cos. has been buying up local newspapers across the country, betting that he can return them to profitability by delegating operations to local staff and building up each paper individually.

Lee owns and operates nearly 350 publications in 72 markets.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Charter's stock just got hammered. Here's what fueled its worst day on record.
data.description:
By Lukas I. Alpert

Charter lost more internet subscribers than expected in the latest quarter, signaling that the company isn't out of the woods when it comes to broadband woes

Charter Communications, which operates its cable and broadband services under the name Spectrum, said it expects its deal to acquire Cox Communications to close by the summer.

It's a tough time to be a cable and broadband operator.

Charter Communications (CHTR) reported steep declines in both its broadband and cable customer bases in the first quarter, sending its stock into a tailspin as it moves to close a $34.5 billion acquisition of Cox Communications.

Shares were down 25% in afternoon trading following the earnings report, in which the company reported a greater-than-expected drop in broadband subscribers as well as lower-than-expected earnings per share. The stock drop is on pace to be Charter's worst daily performance on record.

Shares of cable peer Comcast (CMCSA) are down more than 11% in sympathy, even though that company's own earnings report was well-received a day earlier.

Charter said it ended the quarter with a net 120,000 fewer broadband customers compared with the prior quarter. That was steeper than the 59,000 customers it lost in the same quarter a year earlier and also worse than the estimated decline of 100,000 predicted by analysts.

That decline suggested that Charter still has work to do when it comes to stabilizing its business in the face of changing customer habits.

"Charter may be some ways away from adding subscribers, but a reasonable objective should be fewer year-on-year net losses," analyst Craig Moffett of MoffettNathanson Research wrote in a note to clients. "They're not there yet."

Traditional cable companies have struggled as AT&T (T), Verizon Communications (VZ) and T-Mobile US (TMUS) encroach on their territory with internet offerings at both the high and low ends. While Charter is pushing to bundle wireless service with its more established offerings, that part of the business is "growing rapidly, but a little slower than expected," Moffett wrote.

Charter also reported a loss of 60,000 cable subscribers for its Spectrum service in the quarter, which marked an improvement from the 180,000 it lost in the same quarter in 2025. The company attributed that to "simplified pricing and packaging and benefits from the inclusion of programmers' streaming applications in Spectrum's expanded basic video packages."

The company said that it expected its average revenue per user - a key industry metric - to be flat for the year.

Charter remains the largest cable provider in the country, with 12.5 million subscribers as of the end of the first quarter, but industry watchers expect it to soon be surpassed by YouTube TV, which had an estimated 10 million subscribers late last year.

The company reported revenue of $13.6 billion in the quarter, down 1% compared with the same period of the previous year but in line with expectations. The company posted net income of $1.16 billion in the quarter, down from $1.22 billion the year earlier. That translated to earnings per share of $9.17, below the $9.96 predicted by analysts.

The disappointing earnings report comes as Charter moves toward closing the $34.5 billion deal it reached last year to acquire Cox Communications.

"We've now received all the necessary federal and state approvals that we need to close, except from California, and we're working with the California Public Utilities Commission towards the summer close," Charter CEO Christopher Winfrey said on a call with analysts.

The deal comes at a time of deep unsteadiness for the cable industry, as customers are increasingly cutting the cord and moving to streaming video services. There are now about 60 million cable subscribers in the U.S., down from around 100 million a decade ago.

-Lukas I. Alpert

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Gaming & Leisure Properties posts record Q1 2026 revenue $420.0M, raises AFFO guidance
data.description:
Gaming & Leisure Properties reported record first-quarter 2026 results, with total revenue of $420.0 million and net income of $239.4 million for the three months ended March 31, 2026. The company reported FFO of $304.0 million and AFFO of $297.1 million and raised 2026 AFFO per share guidance to $4.08–$4.12. Management highlighted accretive portfolio additions and ongoing development funding activity supporting near-term growth.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $420.0 million for the three months ended March 31, 2026 (vs. $395.2 million in Q1 2025).
  • Income from operations: $333.3 million for Q1 2026 (vs. $258.8 million in Q1 2025).
  • Net income: $239.4 million for Q1 2026 (vs. $170.4 million in Q1 2025); diluted net income per share: $0.82 (Q1 2025: $0.60).
  • FFO: $304.0 million (FFO per diluted share: $1.04); AFFO: $297.1 million (AFFO per diluted share: $1.02).
  • Adjusted EBITDA: $393.0 million; company raised full-year 2026 AFFO guidance to $1.212–$1.223 billion, or $4.08–$4.12 per diluted share and OP/LTIP units.

Business Highlights

  • Completed two accretive transactions totaling $727 million: acquisition of Bally’s Twin River Lincoln real estate assets for $700 million and purchase of land for Cordish Live! Virginia for $27 million; both at an 8.0% cap rate.
  • Funded approximately $159 million toward development projects in Q1, including ongoing funding for Bally's Chicago and other development commitments.
  • Development pipeline remains significant with nearly $1.8 billion of commitments remaining to fund and planned additional development fundings of $590–$640 million through the remainder of 2026.
  • Balance sheet and capital activity: issued $800 million of senior notes due 2036 at a 5.625% coupon; reported net leverage of 5.0x net debt to adjusted EBITDA as of March 31, 2026 (at the low end of the 5.0x–5.5x target range).
  • Portfolio and geographic expansion: portfolio interests in 71 gaming and related facilities across 21 states; entry into Virginia represents the 21st state for the portfolio.

Original SEC Filing: Gaming & Leisure Properties, Inc. [ GLPI ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Farmer Bros. clarifies 401(k) proxy voting procedure for merger special meeting
data.description:
Farmer Bros. clarified that 401(k) Plan shares without timely voting instructions will be voted proportionally to instructed shares.

Key Highlights:

  • 401(k) participants retain the right to direct how their shares are voted for the special meeting.
  • Shares without timely directions will be voted by the 401(k) Trustee in proportion to shares with timely instructions.
  • Voting directions must be received by 11:59 p.m. ET on April 28, 2026 to be timely.
  • This clarification supplements the definitive proxy for the proposed merger with Royal Cup and Merger Sub.

Original SEC Filing: FARMER BROTHERS CO [ FARM ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026

data.title: Coterra Energy receives demand letters, supplements proxy with Goldman Sachs valuation details
data.description:
Coterra Energy supplemented its joint proxy with Devon, disclosing Goldman Sachs valuation analyses and responding to stockholder demand letters related to the merger.

Key Highlights:

  • Received multiple demand letters alleging disclosure deficiencies and voluntarily provided supplemental disclosures to avoid delays.
  • Goldman Sachs DCF analysis implies present value per Coterra share of $25.43–$31.86 using terminal EBITDA ~$5,202m.
  • Goldman Sachs' present-value analyses produce ranges: company stand-alone $24.05–$30.47; pro forma exchange ratio implied $27.95–$38.51.
  • Special stockholder meetings for Coterra and Devon remain scheduled for May 4, 2026; supplemental disclosures do not change meeting timing.

Original SEC Filing: Coterra Energy Inc. [ CTRA ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Consumer Portfolio Services Securitizes $526M Receivables, Issues $514M Auto ABS 2026-B
data.description:
Consumer Portfolio Services completed a securitization of approximately $526.17 million of subprime auto receivables and issued $514.07 million of asset-backed notes through CPS Auto Receivables Trust 2026-B. The five-note classes carry fixed coupons from 4.35% to 7.14%, supported by 1.00% cash reserve and 2.30% initial overcollateralization, with targeted enhancement levels over time. CPS remains servicer, while Computershare Trust Company acts as trustee, collateral agent and backup servicer. The transaction provides long-term funding and is treated as secured financing for accounting and tax purposes.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Auto receivables securitization and issuance of $514.07M asset-backed notes
  • Counterparty: CPS Auto Receivables Trust 2026-B; Computershare Trust Company
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 22 2026 / Apr 22 2026
  • Duration / Termination: N/A
  • Reason: Access funding and optimize cost of capital

Original SEC Filing: CONSUMER PORTFOLIO SERVICES, INC. [ CPSS ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Bayview Acquisition Announced Transfer or Voluntary Withdrawal of Listing
data.description:
On April 22, 2026, a Nasdaq Hearings Panel granted the company continued listing subject to conditions, and effective April 24, 2026 its securities were transferred from the Nasdaq Global Market to the Nasdaq Capital Market. The company must close its business combination with Oabay by June 19, 2026 and meet initial listing rules; trading continues under BAYA, BAYAU and BAYAR.

data.title: Alpine expands ATM programs, adds agents for $150M common and $35M preferred offerings
data.description:
Alpine announced additions to its at-the-market programs, adding sales agents for a $35,000,000 preferred and $150,000,000 common offering capacity.

Key Highlights:

  • Added Cantor and Huntington as sales agents for the 8.00% Series A preferred ATM offering, up to $35,000,000.
  • Updated existing preferred distribution agreements to reflect Cantor and Huntington participation, preserving prior terms.
  • Expanded common-stock ATM program to include AGP, Colliers, Cantor, Huntington, Lucid and UBS as agents and forward counterparties for up to $150,000,000.
  • Amended existing common distribution agreements to accommodate the new agents and forward arrangements; no sale implied by filing.

Original SEC Filing: Alpine Income Property Trust, Inc. [ PINE ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026

data.title: AEVEX Secures $375 Million Senior Secured Credit Facilities Led by Bank of America
data.description:
AEVEX entered into a new $375 million senior secured credit package led by Bank of America, comprising a $100 million term loan, a $75 million delayed draw term loan, and a $200 million revolving facility, maturing April 20, 2031. In connection with the new financing, AEVEX repaid and terminated its March 18, 2020 credit agreement with Ankura Trust, PNC Bank, and other lenders on April 20, 2026. The new facilities include leverage and interest coverage covenants and are secured by substantially all assets, positioning the company with greater liquidity and flexibility for growth.

New agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Senior secured credit facilities (Term Loan, Delayed Draw Term Loan, Revolving Credit Facility)
  • Counterparty: Bank of America, as administrative agent, and other lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 20 2026 / Apr 20 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until Apr 20 2031
  • Reason: Refinance debt and enhance liquidity for growth

Terminated agreement details:

  • Agreement terminated: Senior secured credit agreement
  • Counterparty: Ankura Trust Company as administrative agent; PNC Bank as revolving and collateral agent; and lenders
  • Original agreement date: Mar 18 2020
  • Termination date: Apr 20 2026
  • Termination type: Early
  • Reason: Replaced by new credit facilities

Original SEC Filing: AEVEX Corp. [ AVEX ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Nvidia Stock Logs First Record Close Since October — WSJ
data.description:
By Jared Mitovich

A tech rally led by Intel helped bring Nvidia to its first record close since Oct. 29.

The chip maker closed up 4.3% to $208.26, after Intel reported strong earnings that lifted the semiconductor sector across the board. Nvidia's strong performance on Friday caps the tech giant's resurgence from a turbulent few months in the market that saw the stock skid as much as 5.5% in a day.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: S&P revises Finland's outlook to 'negative' from 'stable' on fiscal, economic risks
data.description:
Credit ratings agency S&P revised Finland's outlook to "negative" from "stable" on Friday, citing persistent risks to Finnish public finances due to low growth, aging demographics and rising defense and interest expenditure.

The agency maintained its ratings for Finland at "AA+/A-1+".

data.title: Procter & Gamble reports $21.24B quarterly sales and $1.63 diluted EPS
data.description:
Procter & Gamble reported net sales of $21,235 million for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, up 7% year over year, with diluted earnings per share of $1.63. Nine‑month results show net sales of $65,828 million and diluted EPS of $5.36, with net earnings of $13,063 million year to date. Management cites volume, pricing and foreign exchange as contributors to revenue growth while productivity and cost actions supported cash generation.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $21,235 million (three months ended Mar 31, 2026), up 7% vs prior year; $65,828 million (nine months), up 4%.
  • Cost of products sold: $10,722 million (three months); $32,442 million (nine months).
  • Operating income: $4,576 million (three months); $15,798 million (nine months). Reported operating margin: 21.5% (three months), 24.0% (nine months).
  • Net earnings: $3,951 million (three months); $13,063 million (nine months), increases of 4% and 5% respectively.
  • Diluted net earnings per common share: $1.63 (three months); $5.36 (nine months). Core EPS: $1.59 (three months); $5.46 (nine months).
  • Cash generation: Operating cash flow $14,425 million year to date; adjusted free cash flow $11,727 million year to date; productivity 92% (both nine months).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Growth in the quarter and year to date was driven by foreign exchange, increased volumes and pricing.
  • Brand and category momentum: Strength led by Beauty, Health Care and Grooming categories, with particular innovation momentum in SK‑II and Hair Care.
  • Channel and regional performance: E‑commerce and innovation-led gains across Europe, Latin America and Greater China.
  • Operational productivity: Manufacturing productivity improvements and cost savings initiatives helped offset margin pressure from mix, strategic investments and tariffs.
  • Restructuring and portfolio actions: Ongoing portfolio streamlining with a $1.5–$2.0 billion restructuring plan and the dissolution of the Glad joint venture.

Original SEC Filing: PROCTER & GAMBLE Co [ PG ] - 10-Q - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Meritage Homes 10-Q: Q1 Revenue $1,117.2M, EPS $0.82
data.description:
Meritage Homes reported first-quarter closing revenue of $1,117.2 million and diluted earnings per share of $0.82, with net earnings of $55.3 million reflecting lower closings and margin pressure. Revenue declined 17.5% year over year and gross margin contracted amid higher incentives and reduced fixed-cost leverage. The results reflect softer demand and operational actions to shorten cycle times and control discretionary costs.

Financial Highlights

  • Total closing revenue: $1,117.2 million, down 17.5% year over year (home closing revenue $1,107.8 million).
  • Total closing gross profit: $193.5 million; gross margin 17.5% (margin decline driven by higher incentives and lower volume).
  • Homebuilding segment operating income: $75.9 million (reflects lower closings and reduced fixed-cost leverage).
  • Net earnings: $55.3 million, down from $122.8 million in the prior year period.
  • Diluted net income per share: $0.82 per share.

Business Highlights

  • Home closing performance: Q1 closings fell 13.1% to 2,967 homes, contributing to the decline in closing revenue.
  • Orders and backlog: Orders decreased 5.5% to 3,664 units and backlog units declined 6.9% to 1,865, although backlog conversion rates improved.
  • Product and channel strategy: Continued emphasis on all-spec, move-in ready homes with a 60-day closing focus, producing shorter cycle times of under 110 days and supporting a higher community count.
  • Operational efficiency and cost actions: Construction times improved and the company implemented direct cost savings and cuts to discretionary spending to help preserve margins amid higher lot costs.
  • Market demand and incentives: Demand softened with higher cancellations (11%) and increased use of incentives to retain buyers and support affordability.

Original SEC Filing: Meritage Homes CORP [ MTH ] - 10-Q - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Kinder Morgan 10-Q: Revenue $4.83B, EPS $0.44
data.description:
Kinder Morgan reported quarterly revenue of $4,828 million and basic and diluted earnings per share of $0.44, driven by higher natural gas sales and services that lifted consolidated results. Net income attributable to Kinder Morgan rose alongside operating income, reflecting stronger margins and volumes in the natural gas pipelines business. The company also highlighted ongoing investments and an announced Monument Pipeline acquisition to support growth in Houston gas infrastructure.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $4,828 million, up $587 million (14%) year-over-year.
  • Operating Income: $1,444 million, up $299 million (26%) year-over-year.
  • Net Income: $1,001 million, up $258 million (35%) year-over-year.
  • Net Income Attributable to Kinder Morgan: $976 million, up $259 million (36%) year-over-year.
  • Basic and Diluted Earnings Per Share: $0.44, up $0.12 (38%) year-over-year.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth Drivers: Consolidated revenue growth was driven primarily by higher natural gas sales and increased services volumes.
  • Natural Gas Pipelines: This segment led operational growth with higher sales margins, increased volumes and stronger demand contributing materially to EBDA improvement.
  • Products & Terminals: Products Pipelines and Terminals segments saw momentum from wider spreads, higher rates, contributions from expansions and early termination payments.
  • Investments and Acquisitions: The company expects approximately $3.9 billion in investments through 2026 and announced a $505 million Monument Pipeline acquisition to expand Houston-area gas infrastructure.
  • Operational Performance & Hedging: CO2 and Energy Transition Ventures had mixed operational outcomes; the company used CO2 hedges and RNG/RIN production to manage near-term commodity exposure.

Original SEC Filing: KINDER MORGAN, INC. [ KMI ] - 10-Q - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Baker Hughes 10-Q: Revenue $6,587M, EPS $0.93
data.description:
Baker Hughes reported consolidated revenue of $6,587 million for the quarter, a 2% increase year-over-year, while diluted income per Class A common stock was $0.93 compared with $0.40 in the prior-year quarter. The firm cited gains on dispositions and improved EBITDA as drivers of a stronger net income outcome. Operational mix shifts and portfolio transactions materially influenced segment performance during the period.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $6,587 million, up $160 million (2%) versus Q1 2025.
  • Net income: $938 million, up $529 million versus Q1 2025, driven by gains on dispositions and improved EBITDA.
  • Diluted income per Class A common stock: $0.93, versus $0.40 in Q1 2025.
  • Operating income (proxy): $1,274 million income before income taxes, increased versus the prior year.

Business Highlights

  • Segment performance: Industrial & Energy Technology (IET) grew 14% year-over-year driven by Gas Turbomachinery & Services (GTS) and Gas Technology & Equipment (GTE); Oilfield Services & Equipment (OFSE) declined 7% due to the SPC disposition and softness in the Middle East.
  • Portfolio and channel shifts: Major portfolio changes from the PSI sale and the SPC disposition and the formation of an SPC joint venture with Cactus reshaped the company’s segment mix toward gas and climate technology businesses.
  • Market momentum: Strength noted in LNG, gas infrastructure and climate technology markets, supporting IET positioning and demand for related solutions.
  • Operational milestones and proceeds: Completed transactions included $1.2 billion of proceeds from the PSI sale and $0.2 billion from the SPC joint venture; remaining performance backlog (RPO) stood at $36.1 billion with IET holding $33.1 billion.
  • Capital markets activity: Completed a large notes offering to fund the Chart acquisition, reflecting ongoing strategic reshaping of the portfolio.

Original SEC Filing: Baker Hughes Co [ BKR ] - 10-Q - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq close at records on tech lift, Iran peace talk hopes
data.description:
Key points:
  • Intel shares close at record after upbeat Q2 forecast
  • Justice Department to end probe into Powell
  • Semiconductor index notches 18th straight session of gains
  • Indexes: Dow off 0.16%, S&P 500 up 0.8%, Nasdaq up 1.63%

By Chuck Mikolajczak and Niket Nishant

The S&P 500 and Nasdaq closed at record highs on Friday, bolstered by optimism for possible negotiations between the U.S. and Iran to end their war and a surge in Intel shares that extended the rally in semiconductor stocks.

Pakistani government sources said Iran's foreign minister, Abbas Araqchi, was expected in the Pakistani capital, Islamabad, on Friday to discuss proposals for restarting peace talks.

In addition, White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said in an interview with Fox News that President Donald Trump's special envoy Steve Witkoff and son-in-law Jared Kushner will travel to Islamabad on Saturday morning for talks with Iran mediated by Pakistan.

Markets had rallied in recent weeks on hopes that a resolution to the Iran war was on the horizon, along with expectations of strong corporate earnings, but gains have been tempered this week as optimism for a peace deal dimmed, with the Strait of Hormuz remaining shuttered.

"The Iran thing feels kind of tenuous, we've had a lot of back and forth. I assume that will continue, but for now, some rays of sunlight," said Jed Ellerbroek, portfolio manager at Argent Capital Management in St. Louis, Missouri.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 79.61 points, or 0.16%, to 49,230.71, the S&P 500 gained 56.68 points, or 0.80%, to 7,165.08 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 398.09 points, or 1.63%, to 24,836.60.

For the week, the S&P 500 gained 0.55%, the Nasdaq rose 1.5%, and the Dow fell 0.44%.

Semiconductors, one of the market's strongest performers on the year, continued to rally. The Philadelphia SE Semiconductor Index advanced 4.32% to extend its record run of gains to 18 consecutive sessions.

Intel surged 23.65% to close at a record $82.57 and was the best performer on the benchmark S&P index, following a better-than-expected revenue forecast for the second quarter.

"All the doubts and fears about the (return on investment) on the AI CapEx from the big tech companies - Amazon and Google and Microsoft and Meta - those concerns are fading real fast, and that's propelling the chip stocks and the contractors and all the industrial companies," said Ellerbroek.

Fellow chipmakers AMD and Arm both shot higher by about 14%. Megacap Nvidia climbed 4.32% and also closed at a record as it neared the $5 trillion market valuation again.

The S&P 500 technology index rose 2.46% and was the best-performing of the 11 major S&P sectors. Tech stocks also managed to shrug off DeepSeek's preview of its highly awaited new model.

The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq recorded a fourth consecutive week of gains, their longest streak since the fourth quarter of 2024. The Dow, however, snapped a three-week run higher.

FED MEETING AWAITED

Attention is also shifting to the Federal Reserve meeting next week, which will be scrutinized for clues on rate cuts and the central bank's leadership succession.

The U.S. Justice Department is closing its investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell, clearing an obstacle to the confirmation of Kevin Warsh, Trump's pick to lead the central bank.

Markets were pricing in a roughly 39% chance for a cut of at least 25 basis points at the Fed's December meeting, according to CME's FedWatch Tool, up from about 23% in the prior session.

A strong start to earnings season has helped buttress stocks against volatile Iran news. Earnings growth expectations for the first quarter now stand at 16.1%, according to LSEG data, up from 14.4% at the start of April.

Advancing issues outnumbered decliners by a 1.47-to-1 ratio on the NYSE and by a 1.38-to-1 ratio on the Nasdaq.

The S&P 500 posted 34 new 52-week highs and 8 new lows while the Nasdaq Composite recorded 126 new highs and 90 new lows.

Volume on U.S. exchanges was 17.81 billion shares, compared with the 18.39 billion average for the full session over the last 20 trading days.
